skill.name: data-engineering-data-driven-feature
skill.description: Plan and deliver product features using data analysis, A/B experiment design, analytics instrumentation, rollout monitoring, and post-launch measurement.
skill.summary: Guide data-driven feature development from hypothesis through experiment analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks around a resource path and a YAML configuration example. The weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by analytics and experiment terms such as A/B testing, Bayesian methods, and confidence levels; no cryptographic implementation or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks on SKILL.md line 27 and lines 138-161. Line 27 references a documentation file in inline markdown, and lines 138-161 are a fenced YAML configuration example, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown formatting and a static YAML example. There is no command interpreter, subprocess call, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Experiment Terminology Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged multiple SKILL.md lines as weak cryptography. The cited context discusses A/B testing, Bayesian analysis, confidence levels, analytics platforms, and feature rollout planning, with no hashing, encryption, signing, or password storage logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are used in product experimentation and statistics guidance. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or code that performs cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Teams often ship features without clear evidence, instrumentation, or decision criteria. This skill structures feature work around data analysis, experiment design, implementation, rollout monitoring, and post-launch learning.
actual_capabilities: Breaks feature delivery into analysis, architecture, implementation, validation, launch, and decision phases.
Defines roles for data scientists, analysts, backend engineers, frontend engineers, and deployment engineers.
Guides A/B test planning with sample size, randomization, success metrics, and guardrail metrics.
Specifies analytics instrumentation, event schemas, data pipelines, dashboards, and data quality validation.
Includes rollout controls such as feature flags, traffic allocation, kill switches, monitoring, and rollback criteria.
Supports post-launch analysis with statistical review, business impact assessment, and optimization planning.
limitations: It is a process guide and does not include executable implementation scripts.
It assumes access to analytics, feature flag, monitoring, and data warehouse tools.
It does not calculate sample sizes or statistical significance by itself.
It depends on the user's product context, baseline metrics, and available event data.

use_cases.title: Validate a Product Feature
use_cases.description: Create a measurable hypothesis, define experiment groups, instrument events, and decide whether to ship based on results.

use_cases.title: Design Feature Analytics
use_cases.description: Plan event schemas, funnel tracking, data pipelines, and dashboards before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Launch Safely With Measurement
use_cases.description: Use feature flags, gradual rollout, monitoring, guardrail metrics, and rollback rules for controlled release.

prompt_templates.title: Define the Feature Hypothesis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new feature idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a data-driven feature for: [feature idea]. Help me define the user segment, baseline metric, success metric, guardrail metrics, and a testable hypothesis.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Analytics Instrumentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design analytics instrumentation for: [feature]. Include event names, event properties, funnel steps, user identifiers, and data quality checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Experiment and Rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an A/B experiment for: [feature]. Include sample size inputs, randomization, control and treatment groups, traffic allocation, guardrail metrics, and rollback criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Launch Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze experiment results for: [feature]. Review primary metrics, secondary metrics, segment effects, business impact, risks, and recommend ship, iterate, or rollback.

output_examples.input: Plan an experiment for a new onboarding checklist.
output: A hypothesis connecting checklist completion to activation rate.
A metric plan covering activation, time to first value, retention, and support contacts.
A rollout plan using feature flags, staged traffic, monitoring, and rollback thresholds.

output_examples.input: Design analytics for a recommendation feature.
output: An event taxonomy for impressions, clicks, dismissals, conversions, and feedback.
A data pipeline plan for real-time monitoring and warehouse analysis.
A validation checklist for staging events, user identity, session tracking, and funnel completeness.

output_examples.input: Assess whether to launch an experiment winner.
output: A decision summary comparing primary uplift, confidence intervals, and guardrail movement.
A business impact assessment with expected value and operational cost.
A recommendation to ship, iterate, continue testing, or roll back.

best_practices: Define success metrics and guardrail metrics before implementation starts.
Validate event tracking and experiment assignment in staging before rollout.
Document the final decision, metric results, and lessons for future experiments.
anti_patterns: Do not launch the feature before defining the measurement plan.
Do not change the primary metric after seeing experiment results.
Do not ignore guardrail metrics when the primary metric improves.

faq.question: Does this skill run experiments automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow for planning, implementing, monitoring, and analyzing experiments.

faq.question: Which analytics tools does it support?
faq.answer: It references tools such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment, Snowflake, BigQuery, Datadog, and New Relic as examples.

faq.question: Can it help with feature flags?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes feature flag planning, traffic allocation, kill switches, and gradual rollout guidance.

faq.question: Is it only for A/B testing?
faq.answer: No. It also covers exploratory data analysis, instrumentation, data pipelines, monitoring, and business impact review.

faq.question: Does it provide statistical formulas?
faq.answer: No. It prompts for statistical planning, power analysis, confidence intervals, and testing methods, but does not compute them directly.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Product managers, data engineers, analysts, data scientists, and engineering leads can use it for measured feature delivery.
